game_name: Back to the Future Part II & III

danger_signals.visual_form: anachronistic objects scattered across time periods
danger_signals.meaning: World state corruption - items from wrong eras signal danger to timeline integrity
Primary threat to protagonist and timeline restoration mission

safety_signals.visual_form: item collected and placed in proper time period
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward temporal restoration; incremental world-state healing

safety_signals.visual_form: time period cleared of anachronistic objects
safety_signals.meaning: Temporal safety achieved for that era; reduced world threat

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Biff and temporal obstacles manifest as opposition
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: time machine locations; safe zones tied to Doc Brown presence

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: presence across multiple time periods (1955, 1985, 2015, 1885) signals wide threat scope
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: scattered anachronistic items indicate Biff's disruptive actions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 30 items displaced across three time periods; environmental clutter indicating disorder
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Marty must navigate expanded map spaces, search exhaustively without guidance (no maps/passwords provided), suggesting high cognitive burden and world complexity

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Part III - Temporal Stability (1885 isolated zone)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Smaller map with fewer items; reduced visual/spatial complexity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Simplified navigation and collection mechanics suggest lower threat level and more manageable world state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Anachronistic objects visually present in 'wrong' era environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Collection/return mechanic required; incomplete collection extends mission duration (30-item constraint against time pressure)

environmental_storytelling.example: 1955 items found in 1985/2015 and vice versa; visual evidence of Biff's temporal tampering without explicit exposition
Part II spans three time periods with 30 items = sprawling, chaotic world; Part III single era with fewer items = narrative containment and stabilization
Lack of maps/passwords in Part II signals information loss; world-state confusion mirrored in player experience

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: exploration-driven platforming across expansive maps; time-pressure awareness (30-item quota)
implicit threat through distributed temporal disruption; appears across multiple eras (narrative omnipresence)
remain visually present in wrong time periods; collection behavior required to restore world state

notes: Limited source material (TCRF entry) provides structural/design documentation rather than detailed visual analysis. Manifestation patterns inferred from game design framework: Part II/III structural differences communicate world-state severity through spatial/item complexity. Anachronistic object placement is primary visual language for temporal corruption. Biff's distributed antagonism across eras signals systemic threat. No detailed color palette, sprite variation, or HUD communication documented in available source.
sources_used: tcrf - Back to the Future Part II & III page (design structure, item distribution, era mechanics)
